release.method: GitHub Release -> publish.yml -> OIDC trusted publishing (no token)
verified: build + twine check green in CI
OIDC publisher matched first try (no filename/name mismatch)
fresh venv: pip install gui4gmns (0.1.0) -> import + generate OK
release.testpypi_dry_run: skipped by owner decision (trusted-publishing failures are non-destructive); publish-testpypi.yml remains for future use

prs_merged: #1 Release v0.1.0: verified packaging + release runbook + QA scaffold
#2 Fix: guard-clean the QA report before release tag
next_actions: Phase 2 priority #1: GEN + auto-portals on chicago_sketch (public showcase) — walk checkpoints A/B/C/D/K/L, screenshot evidence
Phase 2 #2: adapters (its_datahub on public sample structure; semidynamic_trajectories auto-synth on Chicago Sketch)
Phase 2 #3: checkpoint-L audit — plant one defect per class on toys, confirm the audit flags each
Consider bumping CI actions off deprecated Node.js 20 (checkout/setup-python/upload-artifact) — cosmetic
notes: Desktop-path venv .exe console shims are blocked by security policy; exercise the CLI via python -c "import sys,gui4gmns; sys.argv=[...]; gui4gmns.main()". Keep private-pattern tokens (e.g. exact decoy filenames) out of tracked QA docs — the guard content-check will flag them.
